findings.title: Persona, principles, and degradation handling align tightly with Slide Architect role
findings.detail: Overview, identity, communication style for agent-to-agent clarity, explicit Does Not Do boundaries, and structured failure modes (API, quality, circuit breaker, missing context) match a specialist who decides parameters and returns structured results. This reads as one coherent product surface rather than a grab bag of prompts.

findings.title: Internal capability table maps cleanly to reference files
findings.detail: PR/SG/QA/ES/CT/SM each route to a single reference doc, which supports progressive disclosure and keeps SKILL.md scannable. External skills are called out in a dedicated table with context passed, which aids integration thinking.

findings.title: Quality rubric matches persona emphasis on honesty and pedagogy
findings.detail: Dimensions (brand, fidelity, layout, accessibility, pedagogical alignment, completeness) and L-level weights mirror principles about physician audiences, embellishment control, and conservative scoring. QA capability substantiates the claimed mastery of output judgment.

findings.title: Memory sidecar design reinforces learning principle without caching the style bible
findings.detail: Default vs ad-hoc write rules, append discipline, condensation guidance, and explicit read/write/deny zones align with principle 6 (learn from runs) and principle 3 (style guide baseline). Reduces risk of stale brand context in memory.

findings.title: Documented execution stack for Gamma API is not present in this workspace
findings.detail: SKILL.md and memory boundaries require skills/gamma-api-mastery (and project api_clients) for all HTTP operations, but the repository only contains minimal gamma-api-mastery content (e.g. doc-sources) and no SKILL.md, parameter-catalog.md, or GammaClient artifacts were found under the workspace root. A user or orchestrator following On Activation and External Skills would hit dead ends for generate/poll/export/download and doc refresh targets.
findings.action: Add the full gamma-api-mastery skill (SKILL.md, parameter catalog, operations scripts) and Gamma client code referenced in references, or add an explicit repo note in SKILL.md when this agent is distributed without the execution bundle.

findings.title: Woodshed integration references evaluator scripts that are absent here
findings.detail: Exemplar study names GammaEvaluator, gamma_evaluator.py, gamma_operations.py, and parameter-catalog refresh paths. Those files are not present in the workspace, so the ES capability and woodshed row in SKILL.md describe a workflow the current tree cannot run. This breaks end-to-end exemplar mastery until the stack exists or docs are scoped to optional.
findings.action: Either implement the referenced scripts under skills/gamma-api-mastery and skills/woodshed integration, or revise exemplar-study.md and SKILL.md external table to state prerequisites and minimal stubs.

findings.title: Style guide write-back routes through an unlisted external skill
findings.detail: SG documents persistence via production-coordination and manage_style_guide.py, but SKILL.md External Skills only lists gamma-api-mastery and woodshed. Users delegating to Gary may not know to invoke production-coordination for approved write-back, creating a hidden dependency in the learning loop.
findings.action: Add production-coordination (or the exact script path) to External Skills with status and context, or fold write-back steps into save-memory.md with explicit orchestration notes for Marcus.

findings.title: Frontmatter trigger emphasizes direct user chat while body centers Marcus delegation
findings.detail: Description invites talking to Gary for slides; Overview states Gary communicates primarily with Marcus and not the user in standard production. The tension is mostly resolved by interactive vs headless sections but can confuse trigger-based routing expectations.
findings.action: Tweak description to mention orchestrated workflows and optional direct woodshed/debug mode so persona and discovery text agree.

findings.title: Parameter merge order is repeated across multiple references
findings.detail: Parameter priority appears in parameter-recommendation.md; style-guide-integration.md repeats a similar layered merge with slightly different ordering labels (API defaults vs style guide). The concepts align but duplication increases drift risk if one file is updated without the other.
findings.action: Define a single canonical merge-order subsection in one reference and have others link to it with one sentence each.

findings.title: Optional single checklist for Marcus delegation handoff
findings.detail: Capabilities chain logically (CT/PR → gamma-api-mastery → QA → SM/SG) but are spread across files. A short numbered checklist in On Activation or Capabilities would strengthen user-journey coherence without adding new capabilities.
findings.action: Add a 6–10 step delegation checklist (envelope parse, style guide + CT, call API skill, export/download, QA block, memory/style write-back) linking to the existing reference anchors.

findings.title: First-run path bridges missing sidecar to concrete next actions
findings.detail: Init specifies sidecar file creation, first interactive messaging, and first headless delegation behavior, which closes an onboarding gap for the memory system and aligns with activation instructions in SKILL.md.

agent_identity.name: bmad-agent-gamma
agent_identity.persona_summary: Gary — Slide Architect and Gamma API parameter specialist for medical education slides, delegated from Marcus, honest about embellishment and layout limits.
agent_identity.primary_purpose: Choose Gamma parameters, invoke API execution via gamma-api-mastery, assess slide quality, persist learnings, and support woodshed exemplar mastery.

persona_alignment.notes: Expertise, communication style, principles, QA rubric, and degradation handling consistently support a parameter-savvy, pedagogy-first specialist.

missing_areas: Runnable gamma-api-mastery skill and client in this tree
GammaEvaluator / operations scripts referenced by exemplar study
Explicit external skill entry for style guide write-back path
capability_completeness.notes: On paper the lifecycle (recommend, generate, assess, export, remember) is complete; in-repo artifacts for API and woodshed execution are the main holes.

consolidation_opportunities.suggested_consolidation: One canonical merge-order doc linked from PR, SG, and CT.

external_integration.notes: gamma-api-mastery and woodshed are first-class; production-coordination appears only in SG write-back and should be surfaced the same way for cohesion.

broken_workflows: Headless production run requiring gamma-api-mastery execution
Woodshed exemplar study requiring GammaEvaluator and related scripts
Style guide persistence after approval without discovering production-coordination
user_journey_score.notes: Documentation supports end-to-end flows; current workspace layout does not fully instantiate them without additional repos or files.

summary.single_most_important_fix: Align the repository with the documented execution stack: ship gamma-api-mastery (plus client and parameter catalog) and woodshed evaluator hooks, or narrow SKILL.md and references to the subset that is actually present.
summary.assessment: The agent reads as a coherent specialist on the page—persona, principles, routing, and QA fit together well—but cohesion with the rest of the repo is weak because critical external paths are missing or unnamed, which fragments real end-to-end journeys.
